answer:
**Work:** *Bang-E-Dara Part 3* by Allama Iqbal  
**Chapter:** 2, *Bilad-e-Islamiya*[^1]

The relevant verse passage is:

> The realm of Constantinople, once the abode of Caesar,[^2]  
> Now an eternal symbol of the Ummah's power and grandeur.[^2]  
> ...  
> Its very air is as pure as the fragrance of a rose;[^3]  
> From the tomb of Ayyub Ansari, a voice arises.[^3]  
>   
> "O Muslim! This city is the very heart of the Islamic nation;[^4]  
> It is the harvest of centuries of struggle and sacrifice."[^4]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, Chapter 2: *Bilad-e-Islamiya* [/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/].
[^2]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, Chapter 2: *Bilad-e-Islamiya* [/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-e0bfb3].
[^3]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, Chapter 2: *Bilad-e-Islamiya* [/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-f14b70].
[^4]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, Chapter 2: *Bilad-e-Islamiya* [/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-630e3b].
